title: (No AI) OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Claude Code
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/Screenshot-2026-04-16-at-13.01.11.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,5.8356559824806,100,89.31166633486)

Codex can control apps on your desktop like Tic Tac Toe. | Image: OpenAI

OpenAI is beefing up its agentic coding and development system, Codex, with [a suite of updates](https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/) that let it use your computer, generate images, and remember from past experiences. The package of updates comes as OpenAI's rivalry with Anthropic intensifies, following the [stellar successes of Claude Code](https://www.theverge.com/report/874308/anthropic-claude-code-opus-hype-moment) and OpenAI [aggressively shifting resources](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911118/openai-memo-cro-ai-competition-anthropic) to catch up.

Codex will now be able to operate desktop apps on your computer, OpenAI [says in a blog post announcing the update](https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/). It can work in the background, meaning it won't interfere with your own work in other apps, and multiple agents can work in parallel. For developers, OpenAI says, "this is he …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/913034/openai-codex-updates-use-macos)

Codex can control apps on your desktop like Tic Tac Toe. | Image: OpenAI	

OpenAI is beefing up its agentic coding and development system, Codex, with a suite of updates that let it use your computer, generate images, and remember from past experiences. The package of updates comes as OpenAI's rivalry with Anthropic intensifies, following the stellar successes of Claude Code and OpenAI aggressively shifting resources to catch up.
Codex will now be able to operate desktop apps on your computer, OpenAI says in a blog post announcing the update. It can work in the background, meaning it won't interfere with your own work in other apps, and multiple agents can work in parallel. For developers, OpenAI says, "this is he …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The package of updates comes as OpenAI's rivalry with Anthropic intensifies, following the stellar successes of Claude Code and OpenAI aggressively shifting resources to catch up. […]

title: (No AI) Google’s AI Mode update lets you open links without leaving the page
author: Emma Roth
content:
![A screenshot of AI Mode with a tab open alongside it](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/google-ai-mode-side-by-side.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google is [upgrading AI Mode in Chrome](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/search/ai-mode-chrome/) with a new feature that will allow you to open links to sources alongside your chat. Now, instead of automatically opening a new tab, clicking a source will open the website side by side with AI Mode, allowing you to ask follow-up questions about what's on the page.

Launched last year, [AI Mode is the chatbot-like search feature](https://www.theverge.com/news/624064/google-ai-mode-overviews-search) that lives within a tab on the left side of Google's search engine. Google has added several new features to the tool over the past year, including the ability to [generate images of outfits and decor](https://www.theverge.com/news/712924/google-shopping-ai-mode-fake-clothes) based on your descriptions, [visualize your travel plans](https://www.theverge.com/news/822240/google-ai-mode-travel-plans-canvas), and find [restaurant res …](https://www.theverge.com/news/763367/google-search-ai-mode-agentic-restaurant-bookings)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/913109/google-ai-mode-tabs-sources)

Launched last year, AI Mode is the chatbot-like search feature that lives within a tab on the left side of Google's search engine. Google has added several new features to the tool over the past year, including the ability to generate images of outfits and decor based on your descriptions, visualize your travel plans, and find restaurant res …

creator: Joel Khalili
title: (No AI) The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund
dc:creator: Joel Khalili
content: In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.
contentSnippet: In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.
